blurb: A graceful native of dappled woodland: arching, unbranched leafy stems tipped with sprays of small white stars in spring, then green berries striped like tiny watermelons that ripen wine-red. A spreading native groundcover for the dry shade under trees.
water: Low to moderate; drought-tolerant in shade once established
spread: Spreads by rhizomes into open colonies — a native groundcover for dappled and dry shade
seasons: Arching unbranched leafy stems; sprays of small white star flowers in spring; striped green berries ripening dark red in late summer; gold foliage in fall
wildlife: Spring flowers for small pollinators; berries eaten by birds and small mammals; woodland cover
toxic: Edible in part but with cautions — the ripe berries are mildly laxative in quantity (see Edible parts)
